A blockchain isn’t secured by miners alone.

It is secured by:

• Miners
• Pool operators
• Explorers
• Exchanges
• Wallet developers
• Community builders

Interchained is investing in the entire ecosystem.

That’s how networks survive.

Most projects ask:

“Who wants to buy?”

We’re asking:

“Who wants to build?”

Pool operators.
Infrastructure providers.
Developers.
Community leaders.

The Interchained Infrastructure Grants Program is now live.

Build with us.

RT @UsamaBadamasi: Why I Think Every Serious Builder Should Consider Owning Their Own AI Platform

Hey, let’s be real for a second.

In 202…

Built agent systems this year while simultaneously:
• running a salon • rebuilding branding • handling operations • doing marketing • architecting infrastructure • shipping real products
Builders in 2026 don’t fit neatly into one category anymore.

Most AI products:
“Here’s a smarter textbox.”
What we’re building:
Systems that can actually DO work.
Huge difference.
Chat is the interface.
Execution is the product.

The biggest misconception in AI right now:
People think the moat is the model.
The moat is:
• orchestration • workflows • tooling • operational reliability • execution safety • product integration
That’s where the real company gets built.

Built systems this year that:
• rewrite stale SEO content safely • moderate communities autonomously • orchestrate multiple AI providers • patch codebases with structured diffs • run long-lived execution workflows
We are way past “write me a poem.” 😂

One of my favorite 2026 builds:
An AI SEO remediation engine inside WordPress that:
• audits pages • grades SEO signals • generates safe patches • processes jobs in parallel • self-heals worker failures
Built originally for a salon business 😭🔥

I now run the Aveda salon on Park Ave in Winter Park, FL. Like a lot of small businesses, our blog had become a graveyard … 80+ posts written over the years, half missing meta descriptions, focus keywords, schema, internal links, or even decent titles.

I didn’t want to pay an agency thousands for a generic “SEO audit,” and I didn’t trust existing AI SEO plugins not to nuke indexed URLs.

So I built my own system: Mint AI Studio.

What it does:
• Crawls every post and grades it A–F on a transparent rubric (title length, meta description, keyword presence, schema, internal links, etc.)
• Shows exactly what’s missing and exactly what it would change
• Lets me bulk-select weak pages and run AI-assisted fixes automatically

Newest feature:
An Auto Apply Queue.

You select all D/F pages, hit Start, close the tab, and it keeps running in the background.

Under the hood:
• Dedicated queue table with atomic SQL claims
• Real worker state machine (idle / running / paused / stopped)
• Parallel workers (1–5 configurable)
• InnoDB row locks to prevent duplicate processing
• Self-spawning non-blocking workers
• WP-Cron only used as a watchdog/recovery layer

Important design choice:
Slug rewrites are OFF by default.

Changing indexed URLs casually is how small businesses accidentally destroy traffic. Instead, the system improves titles, metas, intros, schema, keywords, and internal linking without touching URLs unless explicitly enabled.

Also:
• AI calls are fully server-side
• Browser never sees API keys
• Runs on WordPress 6.x + PHP 8.x + MySQL/MariaDB (InnoDB)

It’s currently wired to AiAssist as the provider, and the UI is branded around the salon aesthetic, but the architecture itself is provider-agnostic.

Honestly curious:
What are other small businesses doing with stale content right now?
